subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Guide the Fraction Compass crew as they match equivalent paths and compare fractions on bars, circles, and number lines.
lesson.mini_lesson: Equivalent fractions name the same point or same amount. To compare fractions, use equal wholes and attend to same numerators or same denominators.
lesson.objective: Recognize equivalent fractions and compare fractions with the same numerator or denominator.
lesson.concept_explanation: Fractions such as 1/2 and 2/4 are equivalent because they cover the same amount. With the same denominator, the larger numerator is larger. With the same numerator, the smaller denominator makes larger parts.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may think different numbers cannot be equal, treat larger denominators as larger fractions, focus only on numerators, or misplace fractions on a number line.
lesson.real_world_connection: Comparing fractions helps with recipes, distances, and sharing fairly.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use models to find equivalent fractions, then compare same denominators, same numerators, or positions on a number line.

hints: Use equal-sized parts or the number line.
Same denominator: compare numerators. Same numerator: the smaller denominator is larger.
Use <, >, or = to compare.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model, then try again.
